releases.description:
# Changelog

### Improvements

- Updated dependencies to address security vulnerabilities (handlebars, picomatch, brace-expansion)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Management-Obsidian/compare/2.0.12...2.0.13

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Improvements

- Updated dependencies to address security vulnerabilities (ajv, minimatch, flatted)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Management-Obsidian/compare/2.0.11...2.0.12

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

###  New Features

- **Hierarchical checkbox reordering**: Checkboxes now move together with their indented content (sub-tasks, descriptions, paragraphs) as unified blocks, preserving the structure of nested tasks (fixes #3, #5) 

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed an issue where numbered checklists reordering wouldn't sync to the correct number after a change.

### Improvements

- The "Start numbering from 1" setting is now properly disabled (grayed out) when auto-renumbering is turned off - Updated dependencies

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Management-Obsidian/compare/2.0.10...2.0.11

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed an issue where drag and drop functionality wasn't working properly
- Resolved a problem with smart pasting on indented lists

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Management-Obsidian/compare/2.0.9...2.0.10

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Pasting no longer moves the cursor to the end of the line.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Management-Obsidian/compare/2.0.8...2.0.9

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Upper case characters now detect correctly for deletion.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.7...2.0.8

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Improvements

- Added support for sorting special checkbox characters by ASCII
- Added "Delete checked items" command to remove completed tasks

### Changes

- Choosing whether checked items appear at top or bottom is now a toggle

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed automatic checklist sorting not triggering consistently after typing

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.6...2.0.7

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Improvements 

- Automatic checklist reordering when pasting or dragging content
- New commands to reorder checkboxes (at cursor/selection or entire note)

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed scrolling behavior when reordering via mouse click when the cursor is out of view. It may still partially scroll due to Obsidian's default behavior.
- Fixed mouse input not working as intended due to preceding elements that render as multiple lines.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.5...2.0.6

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

- Checklists now can be sorted either to the top or to the bottom

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.4...2.0.5

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

- When unchecking items, the cursor follows

### Bug Fixes

- Changes made in checklists now ensure the entire list is reordered.
- Fixed a bug where new lines could not be created after checklists. Empty check boxes now do not trigger the reordering.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.3...2.0.4

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

- Restored the renumbering feature

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/2.0.2...2.0.3

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed mismatch between the detected index by the mouse, and the line that will be reordered.
- Updated name and manifest

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Auto-List-Reordering/compare/2.0.1...2.0.2

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed a bug where reordering did not work as intended when checked using the mouse

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

As of Obsidian version 1.8.3, numbered lists are now automatically renumbered as a built-in feature. The feature was removed as it is no longer needed, and the plugin now only supports automatic checkbox reordering.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Automatic-Checklist-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/1.1.2...2.0.0
releases.name: 2.0.0 - Checklist Reordering

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

Checklist sorting fully works, but not integrated as there the option to turn the feature off has not been implemented.
- A final patch before Obsidian version 1.8.3, as numbered lists are now automatically renumbered as a built-in feature.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Automatic-Checklist-Reordering-Obsidian/compare/1.1.2...1.2.0
releases.name: 1.2.0 - Legacy

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Selected text no longer triggers cursor restoration after live update triggers.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/OmriLeviGit/Automatic-Renumbering-Obsidian/compare/1.1.1...1.1.2

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed an issue where the first line in a numbered list would sometimes not automatically renumber.
- Cursor no longer jumps to the beginning of a line when trying to change the numbering.
- Fixed inconsistencies in updating indented numbered lists.

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Changelog

- Plugin title changed to **Automatic List Renumbering**
- Introduced an option to renumber lists so they start from 1. When enabled, all numbered lists will begin at 1.
- Dragging and dropping content now automatically renumbers it, as if it were pasted.

releases.author: OmriLeviGit
releases.description:
# Release 1.0.0 - Initial Release

## Overview
The first official release of the **Automatic Renumbering** plugin for Obsidian. This plugin helps keep your numbered lists in sequential order automatically, which is particularly useful for users who frequently work with structured documents.

## Notable Features
- **Live Updates**: Automatically renumbers lists as you edit, helping maintain accurate sequencing without manual adjustments.
- **Pasting Support**: Ensures correct numbering when pasting content.
- **Manual Control**: Offers commands for manual renumbering if you prefer to manage updates yourself.

## Installation Steps
To get started with the Automatic Renumbering Plugin, follow these steps:
1. Download the latest release files.
2. Create a folder for the files and place that folder in the **plugins folder**. You can find this by opening Obsidian and navigating to   **Options → Community plugins**, where you'll see the folder icon and the **refresh** button next to **Installed plugins**.
3. Click the **refresh** button.
4. Enable the plugin in **Settings > Community plugins**.
5. In the plugin settings, match the tab size to the editor’s tab indent size, which can be found under **Options → Editor → Tab indent size**.

## Usage Commands
You can view the available commands by pressing `Ctrl + P`.
- **Renumber at Cursor**: This command will renumber the list starting from the current cursor position.
- **Renumber Selected Lists**: If you highlight multiple numbered lists, this command renumbers all of them at once.
- **Renumber Entire Note**: This command renumbers every numbered list in your active note.

## Performance
The plugin has been tested with documents containing lists over 10,000 lines and no performance issues were found.

## Limitations
The plugin does not support automatic renumbering in Vim mode, but you can still trigger renumbering commands manually. Additionally, it requires manual specification of tab sizes for correct nested numbering, as automatic detection is not available.

## License Information
This project is licensed under the [MIT License](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT), allowing for both personal and commercial use.
